result.summary: JetBrains exposes product-specific developer surfaces including YouTrack REST API, Hub REST API, TeamCity REST API, Qodana Cloud API, a local JetBrains IDE MCP server, and the TeamCity CLI.
result.description: JetBrains builds developer tools and team products such as IDEs, issue tracking, CI/CD, and code quality services. Its public integration surfaces are documented per product rather than as a single platform-wide API.

youtrack_perm_token.label: YouTrack permanent token
youtrack_perm_token.setup: In YouTrack, open your profile and go to **Account Security**. Click **New token**, choose the needed scope, and copy the token. The developer docs for [Manage Permanent Tokens](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/youtrack/devportal/Manage-Permanent-Token.html) describe the steps and scopes.

youtrack_oauth_client.label: YouTrack OAuth client
youtrack_oauth_client.setup: Have a YouTrack administrator create an OAuth client in YouTrack and configure its flows and redirect URIs. The [OAuth 2.0 Authorization](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/youtrack/devportal/OAuth-authorization-in-youtrack.html) page explains the client setup and notes that starting with YouTrack 2026.2 administrators can manage OAuth clients in YouTrack.

hub_perm_token.label: Hub permanent token
hub_perm_token.setup: In Hub, open your profile, switch to **Account Security/Authentication**, create a new permanent token with the required scope, and copy it. The Hub-facing developer page [Permanent Token Authorization in Hub](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/youtrack/devportal/hub-api-permanent-token.html) points to [Manage Permanent Tokens](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/hub/manage-permanent-tokens.html) for the exact steps.

qodana_org_api_token.label: Qodana organization API token
qodana_org_api_token.setup: In Qodana Cloud, open your organization settings and use the **API token** tab to generate an organization API token. You must have `Owner` or `Admin` access and an eligible license. The [Qodana Cloud API](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/qodana/cloud-api.html) prerequisites section links to the API-token settings.

teamcity_access_token.label: TeamCity access token
teamcity_access_token.setup: Create a personal access token in TeamCity from **My Settings & Tools | Access Tokens**. The REST API docs say to send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token-value>`, and the CLI can also acquire/store it via `teamcity auth login` as described in [Authentication](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/teamcity/teamcity-cli-authentication.html).

teamcity_userpass.label: TeamCity username and password
teamcity_userpass.setup: Use a valid TeamCity user account's username and password. The REST API supports HTTP Basic auth as documented in [TeamCity REST API Reference](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/teamcity/rest/teamcity-rest-api-documentation.html#REST+Authentication).

hub_oauth_service.label: Hub OAuth service credentials
hub_oauth_service.setup: Register your client as a service in Hub, either in the administrative UI or programmatically, to obtain a service ID and secret. The [OAuth 2.0 Authorization in Hub](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/youtrack/devportal/OAuth-authorization-in-hub.html) page explains the supported flows and the service registration process.
